reasoning_chain_text: 1. Cleans human-caused burned-acre values by year and geographic area.
2. Cleans lightning-caused burned-acre values by year and geographic area.
3. Sums the two causes, computes z-scores by geographic area, and finds the largest absolute anomaly.
original_final_question: Which geographic area had the most anomalous year by z-score in terms of total acres burned compared to its historical annual average, and what year was it in? Answer with the geographic area and year, separated by a comma. Write your answer as [ANSWER].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1 cleans human-caused burned-acre values by year and geographic area.
Node 2 cleans lightning-caused burned-acre values by year and geographic area.
Node 3 sums the two causes, computes z-scores by geographic area, and finds the largest absolute anomaly.

areas = sorted({area for rows in human.values() for area in rows})
records = []
for year in sorted(set(human) & set(lightning)):
    row = {'Year': year}
    for area in areas:
        h = human[year].get(area)
        l = lightning[year].get(area)
        row[area] = None if h is None or l is None else h + l
    records.append(row)
df = pd.DataFrame(records)
best_area = None
best_year = None
best_z = -1.0
for area in areas:
    series = pd.to_numeric(df[area], errors='coerce')
    z = (series - series.mean()) / series.std()
    idx = z.abs().idxmax()
    value = float(z.abs().loc[idx])
    if value > best_z:
        best_area = area
        best_year = int(df.loc[idx, 'Year'])
        best_z = value
print(f'{best_area}, {best_year}')

ideal_query.intent: Using <node_1 answer> and <node_2 answer>, which geographic area and year have the largest absolute burned-acre z-score?
